---
title: "Who Votes in Toronto?"
output:
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: fill
    css: |
      @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=DM+Sans:wght@300;400;500;600&family=DM+Serif+Display&display=swap');
      body, .navbar { font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif !important; }
      .navbar { background-color: #1a2744 !important; border: none !important; }
      .navbar-brand { font-family: 'DM Serif Display', serif !important;
                      font-size: 1.2rem !important; color: #f5f4f0 !important; }
      .navbar-text { color: #c8d8f0 !important; font-size: 0.78rem !important; }
      body { background-color: #f5f4f0 !important; }
      .chart-stage { background: #ffffff; border-radius: 8px; }
      .insight-box { background: #1a2744; color: #f5f4f0; border-radius: 8px;
                     padding: 10px 14px; margin-bottom: 6px;
                     font-size: 0.83rem; line-height: 1.65; }
      .insight-box strong { color: #c8d8f0; }
      .insight-sel { border-left: 3px solid #f4a261; }
      .ward-card { background: #f0f4fa; border-radius: 7px; padding: 10px;
                   text-align: center; }
      .ward-card .wnum { font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 700;
                         color: #1a2744; margin: 0; }
      .ward-card .wlbl { font-size: 0.7rem; color: #555; margin: 0; }
      .rank-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px;
                  font-size: 0.82rem; color: #1a2744; margin-bottom: 4px; }
      .rank-label { width: 160px; flex-shrink: 0; color: #333; font-weight: 500; }
      .rank-bar-bg { flex: 1; background: #e8eef6; border-radius: 4px; height: 8px; }
      .rank-bar-fill { height: 8px; border-radius: 4px; background: #4a90c4; }
      .rank-badge { font-weight: 700; color: #1a2744; white-space: nowrap;
                    text-align: right; font-size: 0.82rem; min-width: 120px; }
      select { border-radius: 6px !important; border: 1.5px solid #1a2744 !important;
               font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif !important; }
      .hint-txt { font-size: 0.75rem; color: #999; margin-top: 4px; }
runtime: shiny
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(flexdashboard)
library(shiny)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(leaflet)
library(plotly)
library(sf)
library(RColorBrewer)
```

```{r data, include=FALSE}
# Data cleaning
ward_lookup <- read_excel("25-wardnames-numbers.xlsx")
voter_stats <- read_excel("2023-mayoral-by-election-voter-statistics.xlsx",
  sheet = "2023 Voter Turnout Statisti M")
ward_census <- read_excel("2023-WardProfiles-2011-2021-CensusData.xlsx",
  sheet = "2021 One Variable")
ward_geo    <- read_excel("2023-WardProfiles-GeographicAreas.xlsx")

#Clean ward_lookup
lookup_clean <- ward_lookup
names(lookup_clean) <- c("ward", "ward_name")
lookup_clean <- lookup_clean %>%
  mutate(ward = as.numeric(ward), ward_name = as.character(ward_name)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(ward))
lookup_clean <- bind_rows(tibble(ward = 0, ward_name = "Toronto"), lookup_clean)
lookup_clean <- lookup_clean %>% mutate(ward = as.numeric(ward))

voter_clean <- voter_stats %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(ward = as.character(ward), ward = trimws(ward)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(ward), ward != "", grepl("^[0-9]+$", ward)) %>%
  mutate(
    ward = as.numeric(ward),
    total_eligible_electors = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", total_eligible_electors)),
    number_voted             = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", number_voted))
  ) %>%
  group_by(ward) %>%
  summarise(
    eligible_voters = sum(total_eligible_electors, na.rm = TRUE),
    number_voted    = sum(number_voted, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_voted   = number_voted / eligible_voters * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(lookup_clean, by = "ward") %>%
  select(ward, ward_name, eligible_voters, number_voted, percent_voted)

#Clean voter_stats
toronto_voter <- voter_clean %>%
  summarise(ward = 0, ward_name = "Toronto",
    eligible_voters = sum(eligible_voters, na.rm = TRUE),
    number_voted    = sum(number_voted, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_voted   = number_voted / eligible_voters * 100)
voter_clean <- bind_rows(toronto_voter, voter_clean)

#Clean ward_census
names(ward_census)[1] <- "indicator"
names(ward_census)[2:ncol(ward_census)] <-
  c("toronto", paste0("ward_", 1:(ncol(ward_census) - 2)))

ward_census$indicator <- ward_census$indicator %>%
  iconv(to = "UTF-8") %>%
  chartr("\u2018\u2019\u201C\u201D", "''\"\"", .)

ward_census2 <- ward_census %>%
  filter(!if_all(everything(), is.na)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(indicator)) %>%
  filter(!indicator %in% c("2021 Census","Source:",
    "Statistics Canada, 2021 Census, Custom Tabulations, 2023.",
    "Wards:","25 Ward model","Prepared by:",
    "Strategic Initiatives, Policy & Analysis",
    "City Planning Division, City of Toronto","December, 2023"))

keep_rows <- c(
  "Total - Age","0 to 4 years","5 to 9 years","10 to 14 years",
  "15 to 19 years","20 to 24 years","25 to 29 years","30 to 34 years",
  "35 to 39 years","40 to 44 years","45 to 49 years","50 to 54 years",
  "55 to 59 years","60 to 64 years","65 to 69 years","70 to 74 years",
  "75 to 79 years","80 to 84 years","85 to 89 years","90 years and over",
  "Median age","Non-immigrants","Immigrants",
  "Total - Highest certificate, diploma or degree for the population aged 15 years and over in private households - 25% sample data",
  "No certificate, diploma or degree",
  "High (secondary) school diploma or equivalency certificate",
  "Postsecondary certificate, diploma or degree",
  "Postsecondary certificate or diploma below bachelor level",
  "Apprenticeship or trades certificate or diploma",
  "Non-apprenticeship trades certificate or diploma","Apprenticeship certificate",
  "College, CEGEP or other non-university certificate or diploma",
  "University certificate or diploma below bachelor level",
  "Bachelor's degree or higher","Bachelor's degree",
  "University certificate or diploma above bachelor level",
  "Degree in medicine, dentistry, veterinary medicine or optometry",
  "Master's degree","Earned doctorate",
  "Total - Population aged 15 years and over by Labour force status - 25% sample data",
  "In the labour force","Employed","Unemployed","Not in the labour force",
  "Unemployment rate",
  "Total - Household total income groups in 2020 for private households - 25% sample data",
  "Under $5,000","$5,000 to $9,999","$10,000 to $14,999","$15,000 to $19,999",
  "$20,000 to $24,999","$25,000 to $29,999","$30,000 to $34,999","$35,000 to $39,999",
  "$40,000 to $44,999","$45,000 to $49,999","$50,000 to $59,999","$60,000 to $69,999",
  "$70,000 to $79,999","$80,000 to $89,999","$90,000 to $99,999","$100,000 and over",
  "$100,000 to $124,999","$125,000 to $149,999","$150,000 to $199,999","$200,000 and over")

census_selected <- ward_census2 %>%
  filter(indicator %in% keep_rows) %>%
  select(indicator, toronto, starts_with("ward_")) %>%
  distinct(indicator, .keep_all = TRUE)

census_mat           <- as.data.frame(census_selected, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
rownames(census_mat) <- census_mat$indicator
census_mat$indicator <- NULL
census_clean         <- as.data.frame(t(census_mat), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
census_clean$region  <- rownames(census_clean)
rownames(census_clean) <- NULL
census_clean <- census_clean %>% select(region, everything()) %>%
  mutate(ward = case_when(region == "toronto" ~ 0,
    TRUE ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(str_remove(region, "ward_"))))) %>%
  mutate(across(-c(region, ward), ~ as.numeric(gsub(",", "", .)))) %>%
  select(-region) %>%
  left_join(lookup_clean, by = "ward") %>%
  select(ward, ward_name, everything())

#Combine census_clean categories
census_clean <- census_clean %>%
  transmute(ward, ward_name,
    age_0_to_14  = `0 to 4 years`+`5 to 9 years`+`10 to 14 years`,
    age_15_to_24 = `15 to 19 years`+`20 to 24 years`,
    age_25_to_44 = `25 to 29 years`+`30 to 34 years`+`35 to 39 years`+`40 to 44 years`,
    age_45_to_64 = `45 to 49 years`+`50 to 54 years`+`55 to 59 years`+`60 to 64 years`,
    age_65_plus  = `65 to 69 years`+`70 to 74 years`+`75 to 79 years`+
                   `80 to 84 years`+`85 to 89 years`+`90 years and over`,
    highschool_diploma_or_under =
      `No certificate, diploma or degree`+
      `High (secondary) school diploma or equivalency certificate`,
    edu_college_trades =
      `Postsecondary certificate or diploma below bachelor level`+
      `Apprenticeship or trades certificate or diploma`+
      `Non-apprenticeship trades certificate or diploma`+`Apprenticeship certificate`+
      `College, CEGEP or other non-university certificate or diploma`+
      `University certificate or diploma below bachelor level`,
    edu_bachelor_plus   = `Bachelor's degree or higher`,
    immigrants          = Immigrants,
    non_immigrants      = `Non-immigrants`,
    in_labour_force     = `In the labour force`,
    employed            = Employed, unemployed = Unemployed,
    not_in_labour_force = `Not in the labour force`,
    unemployment_rate   = `Unemployment rate`,
    income_under_30k  = `Under $5,000`+`$5,000 to $9,999`+`$10,000 to $14,999`+
                        `$15,000 to $19,999`+`$20,000 to $24,999`+`$25,000 to $29,999`,
    income_30k_to_59k = `$30,000 to $34,999`+`$35,000 to $39,999`+`$40,000 to $44,999`+
                        `$45,000 to $49,999`+`$50,000 to $59,999`,
    income_60k_to_99k = `$60,000 to $69,999`+`$70,000 to $79,999`+
                        `$80,000 to $89,999`+`$90,000 to $99,999`,
    income_100k_plus  = `$100,000 to $124,999`+`$125,000 to $149,999`+
                        `$150,000 to $199,999`+`$200,000 and over`)

#Clean ward_geo
geo_clean <- ward_geo[-(1:11), ]
names(geo_clean) <- c("ward", "area_sq_km")
geo_clean <- geo_clean %>%
  mutate(ward = as.numeric(ward), area_sq_km = as.numeric(area_sq_km)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(ward))
toronto_area <- geo_clean %>% summarise(ward=0, area_sq_km=sum(area_sq_km,na.rm=TRUE))
geo_clean <- bind_rows(toronto_area, geo_clean) %>%
  left_join(lookup_clean, by="ward") %>% select(ward, ward_name, everything())

#All data merged into 1 dataset
final_data <- voter_clean %>%
  left_join(census_clean, by=c("ward","ward_name")) %>%
  left_join(geo_clean,    by=c("ward","ward_name"))

# ── End Of Data Cleaning ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# CHANGE 1: Added new pct columns for all income/edu/age/immigration sub-categories
scatter_data <- final_data %>%
  filter(ward != 0) %>%
  mutate(
    total_edu             = highschool_diploma_or_under+edu_college_trades+edu_bachelor_plus,
    pct_no_diploma        = ifelse(total_edu>0, highschool_diploma_or_under/total_edu*100, NA),
    pct_college_trades    = ifelse(total_edu>0, edu_college_trades/total_edu*100, NA),
    pct_bachelor_plus     = ifelse(total_edu>0, edu_bachelor_plus/total_edu*100, NA),
    total_income          = income_under_30k+income_30k_to_59k+income_60k_to_99k+income_100k_plus,
    pct_income_under_30k  = ifelse(total_income>0, income_under_30k/total_income*100, NA),
    pct_income_30k_to_59k = ifelse(total_income>0, income_30k_to_59k/total_income*100, NA),
    pct_income_60k_to_99k = ifelse(total_income>0, income_60k_to_99k/total_income*100, NA),
    pct_income_100k_plus  = ifelse(total_income>0, income_100k_plus/total_income*100, NA),
    total_age             = age_0_to_14+age_15_to_24+age_25_to_44+age_45_to_64+age_65_plus,
    pct_age_0_to_14       = ifelse(total_age>0, age_0_to_14/total_age*100, NA),
    pct_age_15_to_24      = ifelse(total_age>0, age_15_to_24/total_age*100, NA),
    pct_age_25_to_44      = ifelse(total_age>0, age_25_to_44/total_age*100, NA),
    pct_age_45_to_64      = ifelse(total_age>0, age_45_to_64/total_age*100, NA),
    pct_age_65_plus       = ifelse(total_age>0, age_65_plus/total_age*100, NA),
    total_imm             = immigrants+non_immigrants,
    pct_immigrants        = ifelse(total_imm>0, immigrants/total_imm*100, NA),
    pct_non_immigrants    = ifelse(total_imm>0, non_immigrants/total_imm*100, NA))

toronto_row <- final_data %>% filter(ward == 0)
avg_turnout <- round(toronto_row$percent_voted, 1)
total_voted <- format(toronto_row$number_voted,    big.mark=",")
total_elig  <- format(toronto_row$eligible_voters, big.mark=",")

# Load ward GeoJSON (must be in same folder as this .Rmd)
map_sf <- tryCatch({
  ws <- sf::st_read("toronto_wards.geojson", quiet=TRUE)
  id_col <- "AREA_SHORT_CODE"
  ws$ward <- as.integer(as.character(ws[[id_col]]))
  ws %>% left_join(scatter_data, by="ward") %>% filter(!is.na(percent_voted))
}, error = function(e) NULL)

# CHANGE 2: get_x now takes attr + sub to support two-level dropdown
get_x <- function(df, attr, sub) {
  switch(paste(attr, sub, sep="_"),
    income_under30k   = list(x=df$pct_income_under_30k,  lbl="% of households earning under $30k"),
    income_100kplus   = list(x=df$pct_income_100k_plus,  lbl="% of households earning $100k+"),
    education_nodip   = list(x=df$pct_no_diploma,        lbl="% of population with no diploma / high school"),
    education_college = list(x=df$pct_college_trades,    lbl="% of population with college / trades"),
    education_bach    = list(x=df$pct_bachelor_plus,     lbl="% of population with bachelor's degree or higher"),
    age_0to14         = list(x=df$pct_age_0_to_14,       lbl="% of population aged 0-14"),
    age_15to24        = list(x=df$pct_age_15_to_24,      lbl="% of population aged 15-24"),
    age_25to44        = list(x=df$pct_age_25_to_44,      lbl="% of population aged 25-44"),
    age_45to64        = list(x=df$pct_age_45_to_64,      lbl="% of population aged 45-64"),
    age_65plus        = list(x=df$pct_age_65_plus,       lbl="% of population aged 65+"),
    immigration_imm   = list(x=df$pct_immigrants,        lbl="% of population who are immigrants"),
    immigration_nonimm= list(x=df$pct_non_immigrants,    lbl="% of population who are non-immigrants")
  )
}

sub_choices <- list(
  income    = c("Under $30k"="under30k","$100k+"="100kplus"),
  education = c("No Diploma / High School"="nodip",
                "College / Trades"="college","Bachelor's+"="bach"),
  age       = c("Age 0-14"="0to14","Age 15-24"="15to24","Age 25-44"="25to44",
                "Age 45-64"="45to64","Age 65+"="65plus"),
  immigration = c("Immigrants"="imm","Non-immigrants"="nonimm")
)

# Helper: compute rank (1 = highest) among all 25 wards
ward_rank <- function(col, val) {
  rank(-col, ties.method="min")[which(round(col, 6) == round(val, 6))][1]
}
```

# Dashboard

## Row {data-height=80}

### {.no-title}
```{r banner}
div(style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;
           padding:16px 28px;background:#1a2744;border-radius:8px;",
  div(style="color:#f5f4f0;",
    tags$p(style="margin:0;font-size:1.2rem;opacity:0.65;letter-spacing:.08em;",
           "CIVIC VITALITY \u00b7 TORONTO 2023 MAYORAL BY-ELECTION"),
    tags$h3(style="margin:0;font-family:'DM Sans',sans-serif;font-weight:600;font-size:1.9rem;letter-spacing:0.01em;",
            "Who Votes in Toronto?")),
  div(style="display:flex;gap:48px;",
    lapply(list(list(paste0(avg_turnout,"%"),"City-wide Turnout"),
                list(total_voted,"Votes Cast"),
                list(total_elig,"Eligible Voters")),
      function(s) div(style="text-align:center;color:#f5f4f0;",
        tags$p(style="margin:0;font-size:1.8rem;font-weight:700;",s[[1]]),
        tags$p(style="margin:0;font-size:1.2rem;opacity:0.65;",s[[2]])))))
```

## Row {data-height=500}

### Toronto Ward Map — Voter Turnout {data-width=480}

```{r map-panel}
selected_ward <- reactiveVal(NULL)
leafletOutput("ward_map", height="100%")
output$ward_map <- renderLeaflet({
  pal <- colorNumeric(c("#e8d5f5","#9b59b6","#4a235a"),
                      domain=scatter_data$percent_voted, na.color="#ccc")
  if (!is.null(map_sf) && nrow(map_sf)>0) {
    lbl <- sprintf(
      "<strong>Ward %s \u2014 %s</strong><br/>Turnout: <strong>%.1f%%</strong><br/>Votes: %s",
      map_sf$ward, map_sf$ward_name,
      map_sf$percent_voted, format(map_sf$number_voted, big.mark=",")) %>%
      lapply(htmltools::HTML)
    leaflet(map_sf) %>%
      addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") %>%
      addPolygons(
        layerId=~ward, fillColor=~pal(percent_voted), fillOpacity=0.8,
        color="#fff", weight=1.5,
        highlightOptions=highlightOptions(
          weight=3,color="#f4a261",fillOpacity=0.95,bringToFront=TRUE),
        label=lbl,
        labelOptions=labelOptions(
          style=list("font-family"="DM Sans,sans-serif","font-size"="12px"),
          direction="auto")) %>%
      addLegend(pal=pal, values=~percent_voted, title="Voter Turnout (%)",
                position="bottomright", labFormat=labelFormat(suffix="%")) %>%
      setView(lng=-79.38, lat=43.72, zoom=10)
  } else {
    ward_centroids <- tibble(ward=1:25,
      lat=c(43.740,43.652,43.614,43.645,43.695,43.705,43.720,43.693,43.668,43.705,
            43.718,43.715,43.686,43.672,43.687,43.702,43.680,43.665,43.648,43.638,
            43.700,43.720,43.755,43.770,43.780),
      lon=c(-79.561,-79.514,-79.482,-79.447,-79.478,-79.400,-79.360,-79.330,-79.300,
            -79.280,-79.259,-79.240,-79.353,-79.384,-79.430,-79.465,-79.388,-79.365,
            -79.372,-79.388,-79.310,-79.296,-79.310,-79.350,-79.390))
    d <- scatter_data %>% left_join(ward_centroids, by="ward")
    lbl <- sprintf(
      "<strong>Ward %s \u2014 %s</strong><br/>Turnout: <strong>%.1f%%</strong>",
      d$ward, d$ward_name, d$percent_voted) %>% lapply(htmltools::HTML)
    leaflet(d) %>%
      addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") %>%
      addCircleMarkers(lng=~lon, lat=~lat, layerId=~ward,
        radius=10, fillColor=~pal(percent_voted), fillOpacity=0.85,
        color="#fff", weight=1.5, label=lbl,
        labelOptions=labelOptions(
          style=list("font-family"="DM Sans,sans-serif"),direction="auto")) %>%
      addLegend(pal=pal, values=~percent_voted, title="Voter Turnout (%)",
                position="bottomright", labFormat=labelFormat(suffix="%")) %>%
      setView(lng=-79.38, lat=43.72, zoom=10)
  }
})
observeEvent(input$ward_map_shape_click, {
  w <- as.numeric(input$ward_map_shape_click$id)
  if (!is.null(selected_ward()) && selected_ward()==w) selected_ward(NULL) else selected_ward(w)
})
observeEvent(input$ward_map_marker_click, {
  w <- as.numeric(input$ward_map_marker_click$id)
  if (!is.null(selected_ward()) && selected_ward()==w) selected_ward(NULL) else selected_ward(w)
})
observeEvent(selected_ward(), {
  sel_w <- selected_ward()
  pal <- colorNumeric(c("#e8d5f5","#9b59b6","#4a235a"),
                      domain=scatter_data$percent_voted, na.color="#ccc")
  if (!is.null(map_sf) && nrow(map_sf)>0) {
    proxy <- leafletProxy("ward_map")
    proxy %>% clearShapes()
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(map_sf))) {
      row     <- map_sf[i, ]
      is_sel  <- !is.null(sel_w) && isTRUE(row$ward == sel_w)
      fc      <- tryCatch(pal(row$percent_voted), error=function(e) "#cccccc")
      if (is.na(fc)) fc <- "#cccccc"
      proxy %>% addPolygons(
        data        = row,
        layerId     = ~ward,
        fillColor   = fc,
        fillOpacity = ifelse(is_sel, 1.0, 0.8),
        color       = ifelse(is_sel, "#f4a261", "#ffffff"),
        weight      = ifelse(is_sel, 3.5, 1.5),
        highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
          weight=3, color="#f4a261", fillOpacity=0.95, bringToFront=TRUE),
        label = htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
          "<strong>Ward %s \u2014 %s</strong><br/>Turnout: <strong>%.1f%%</strong>",
          row$ward, row$ward_name, row$percent_voted)),
        labelOptions = labelOptions(
          style=list("font-family"="DM Sans,sans-serif"), direction="auto"))
    }
  }
})
```

### Voter Turnout vs. Socioeconomic Factors {data-width=520}

```{r scatter-panel}
# CHANGE 3: Two-level dropdown — first select attribute, then sub-category
div(style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;margin-bottom:6px;flex-wrap:wrap;",
  tags$label(style="font-weight:600;color:#1a2744;font-size:1.2rem;white-space:nowrap;",
             "Explore by:"),
  selectInput("attr_select", label=NULL,
    choices=c("Income"="income","Education"="education",
              "Age"="age","Immigration"="immigration"),
    selected="income", width="150px"),
  selectInput("sub_select", label=NULL,
    choices=c("Under $30k"="under30k","$100k+"="100kplus"),
    selected="100kplus", width="180px"))

plotlyOutput("scatter_plot", height="390px")

# Update sub_select choices when attr_select changes
observeEvent(input$attr_select, {
  updateSelectInput(session, "sub_select",
    choices = sub_choices[[input$attr_select]],
    selected = sub_choices[[input$attr_select]][1])
})

output$scatter_plot <- renderPlotly({
  attr  <- input$attr_select
  sub   <- input$sub_select
  sel_w <- selected_ward()
  df    <- scatter_data
  xi    <- get_x(df, attr, sub)
  if (is.null(xi)) return(plotly_empty())
  df$xv <- xi$x
  df    <- df %>% filter(!is.na(xv))
  df    <- df %>% mutate(
    pc = if(!is.null(sel_w)) ifelse(ward==sel_w,"#f4a261","#4a90c4") else "#4a90c4",
    ps = if(!is.null(sel_w)) ifelse(ward==sel_w,14,8) else 9,
    po = if(!is.null(sel_w)) ifelse(ward==sel_w,1.0,0.45) else 0.72)
  fit      <- lm(percent_voted~xv, data=df)
  df$fit   <- predict(fit)
  r_val    <- cor(df$xv, df$percent_voted, use="complete.obs")

  plot_ly(df, x=~xv, y=~percent_voted, type="scatter", mode="markers",
    marker=list(color=~pc,size=~ps,opacity=~po,line=list(color="white",width=1.5)),
    text=~paste0("<b>Ward ",ward," \u2014 ",ward_name,"</b><br>",
                 "Voter Turnout: ",round(percent_voted,1),"%<br>",
                 xi$lbl,": ",round(xv,1),"%"),
    hoverinfo="text", source="scatter") %>%
  add_lines(data=df%>%arrange(xv), x=~xv, y=~fit, inherit=FALSE,
    line=list(color="#1a2744",width=1.8,dash="dot"),
    hoverinfo="none", showlegend=FALSE) %>%
  layout(
    font=list(family="DM Sans,sans-serif",color="#1a2744"),
    xaxis=list(title=list(text=xi$lbl,font=list(size=11)),
               gridcolor="#e8e6e0",zeroline=FALSE),
    yaxis=list(title=list(text="Voter Turnout (%)",font=list(size=11)),
               gridcolor="#e8e6e0",zeroline=FALSE),
    paper_bgcolor="#ffffff", plot_bgcolor="#fafaf8",
    margin=list(l=55,r=20,t=20,b=90),
    annotations=list(
      list(x=0.99,y=0.01,xref="paper",yref="paper",
        text=paste0("r = ",round(r_val,2)), showarrow=FALSE,
        font=list(size=11,color="#999"),xanchor="right",yanchor="bottom"),
      list(x=0.5,y=-0.22,xref="paper",yref="paper",
        text=paste0("<i>X-axis: ",xi$lbl,"</i>"), showarrow=FALSE,
        font=list(size=11,color="#666"),xanchor="center",yanchor="top"))) %>%
  config(displayModeBar=FALSE) %>%
  event_register("plotly_click")
})

observeEvent(event_data("plotly_click", source="scatter"), {
  cd <- event_data("plotly_click", source="scatter")
  if(is.null(cd)) return()
  df <- scatter_data
  xi <- get_x(df, input$attr_select, input$sub_select)
  if (is.null(xi)) return()
  df$xv <- xi$x
  df <- df %>% filter(!is.na(xv))
  m  <- df %>% filter(abs(xv-cd$x)<0.5, abs(percent_voted-cd$y)<0.5)
  if(nrow(m)>0){
    w <- m$ward[1]
    if(!is.null(selected_ward())&&selected_ward()==w) selected_ward(NULL) else selected_ward(w)
  }
})
```

## Row {data-height=370}

### Ward Detail {data-width=480}

```{r ward-detail}
uiOutput("ward_detail")

output$ward_detail <- renderUI({
  sel_w <- selected_ward()
  if(is.null(sel_w)) return(
    div(style="padding:14px;color:#999;font-size:1.5rem;font-style:italic;",
      "\U0001F447 Click any ward on the map to see details.",
      tags$br(),
      tags$span(style="font-size:1.4rem;color:#bbb;","Click the same ward again to deselect.")))

  r <- scatter_data %>% filter(ward==sel_w)
  if(nrow(r)==0) return(NULL)

  # ── Rankings among all 25 wards ──────────────────────────────────────────
  n_wards <- nrow(scatter_data)
  rk_turnout  <- ward_rank(scatter_data$percent_voted,   r$percent_voted)
  rk_income   <- ward_rank(scatter_data$pct_income_100k_plus, r$pct_income_100k_plus)
  rk_edu      <- ward_rank(scatter_data$pct_bachelor_plus,    r$pct_bachelor_plus)
  rk_senior   <- ward_rank(scatter_data$pct_age_65_plus,      r$pct_age_65_plus)
  rk_imm      <- ward_rank(scatter_data$pct_immigrants,       r$pct_immigrants)

  # Helper to build one rank row with a bar
  make_rank_row <- function(label, rk, n=25) {
    pct_bar <- round((n - rk + 1) / n * 100)
    div(class="rank-row",
      tags$span(class="rank-label", paste0(label, ":")),
      div(class="rank-bar-bg",
        div(class="rank-bar-fill", style=paste0("width:",pct_bar,"%;"))),
      tags$span(class="rank-badge", paste0("Rank ", rk, " out of ", n)))
  }

  tagList(
    # ── Header ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    div(style="display:flex;align-items:baseline;gap:8px;margin-bottom:10px;flex-wrap:wrap;",
      tags$h4(style="margin:0;color:#1a2744;",
              paste0("Ward ", sel_w, " \u2014 ", r$ward_name)),
      tags$span(style="font-size:1.4rem;font-weight:700;color:#f4a261;",
                paste0(round(r$percent_voted, 1), "%")),
      tags$span(style="color:#999;font-size:0.8rem;", "voter turnout"),
      tags$span(style="color:#bbb;font-size:0.75rem;",
                paste0("(", format(r$number_voted, big.mark=","), " / ",
                       format(r$eligible_voters, big.mark=","), " eligible)"))),

    # ── Stat cards ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    div(style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr);gap:6px;margin-bottom:10px;",
      lapply(list(
        list(paste0(round(r$pct_income_100k_plus, 1), "%"), "Income $100k+"),
        list(paste0(round(r$pct_bachelor_plus, 1),    "%"), "Bachelor's+"),
        list(paste0(round(r$pct_age_25_to_44, 1),     "%"), "Working Age (25-44)"),
        list(paste0(round(r$pct_immigrants, 1),        "%"), "Immigrants")),
        function(s) div(class="ward-card",
          tags$p(class="wnum", s[[1]]),
          tags$p(class="wlbl", s[[2]])))),

    # ── Rankings ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    div(style="border-top:1px solid #e0e4ec;padding-top:8px;",
      tags$p(style="margin:0 0 6px 0;font-size:1rem;font-weight:600;
                    color:#1a2744;letter-spacing:0.04em;text-transform:uppercase;",
             "Rankings among 25 wards (1 = highest)"),
      make_rank_row("Voter Turnout",    rk_turnout),
      make_rank_row("Income $100k+",    rk_income),
      make_rank_row("Bachelor's+",      rk_edu),
      make_rank_row("Age 65+",          rk_senior),
      make_rank_row("Immigrants",       rk_imm)
    )
  )
})
```

### Key Insights {data-width=520}

```{r insights}
uiOutput("insights_panel")

output$insights_panel <- renderUI({
  attr  <- input$attr_select
  sub   <- input$sub_select
  sel_w <- selected_ward()
  df    <- scatter_data
  xi    <- get_x(df, attr, sub)
  if (is.null(xi)) return(NULL)
  df$xv <- xi$x
  df    <- df %>% filter(!is.na(xv))

  r_val    <- cor(df$xv, df$percent_voted, use="complete.obs")
  dir_lbl  <- ifelse(r_val>0,"positive","negative")
  str_lbl  <- ifelse(abs(r_val)>0.6,"strong",ifelse(abs(r_val)>0.3,"moderate","weak"))
  top_w <- df %>% slice_max(percent_voted,n=1)
  bot_w <- df %>% slice_min(percent_voted,n=1)
  cy    <- mean(df$percent_voted,na.rm=TRUE)
  cx    <- mean(df$xv,na.rm=TRUE)

  i1 <- div(class="insight-box", HTML(paste0(
    "\U0001F4CA <strong>Correlation:</strong> There is a <strong>",
    str_lbl," ",dir_lbl,"</strong> relationship (r\u00a0=\u00a0",round(r_val,2),
    ") between <em>",xi$lbl,"</em> and voter turnout across Toronto's 25 wards.")))

  i2 <- div(class="insight-box", HTML(paste0(
    "\U0001F3C6 <strong>Highest:</strong> Ward ",top_w$ward," (",top_w$ward_name,
    ") at <strong>",round(top_w$percent_voted,1),"%</strong>. &nbsp;",
    "\u2B07\uFE0F <strong>Lowest:</strong> Ward ",bot_w$ward," (",bot_w$ward_name,
    ") at <strong>",round(bot_w$percent_voted,1),"%</strong>.")))

  i3 <- if(!is.null(sel_w)){
    rv <- df %>% filter(ward==sel_w)
    if(nrow(rv)>0){
      dt <- round(rv$percent_voted-cy,1); dx <- round(rv$xv-cx,1)
      st <- ifelse(dt>=0,paste0("+",dt),as.character(dt))
      sx <- ifelse(dx>=0,paste0("+",dx),as.character(dx))
      div(class="insight-box insight-sel", HTML(paste0(
        "\U0001F4CD <strong>Ward ",sel_w," vs. City Avg:</strong> Turnout ",
        round(rv$percent_voted,1),"% (",st,"pp). Selected factor: ",
        round(rv$xv,1),"% (",sx,"pp).")))
    }
  } else {
    div(class="insight-box",style="opacity:0.5;", HTML(
      "\U0001F4CD <strong>Ward comparison:</strong> Select a ward to compare against the city average."))
  }
  tagList(i1, i2, i3)
})
```
